title: CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF variable is not working properly
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF variable is not working properly.  If set, podman info --debug shows that store configFile is the one specified by the environment variable, but the contents of the file are ignored and graphRoot and runRood are not changed at all and the default is shown.

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Copy the default storage file for rootless podman to another place

2. Change the graphRoot and runRoot in that file to another different path

3. Run: CONTAINERS_STORAGE_CONF=path_to_your_storage_file podman info —debug

**Describe the results you received:**

The store configFile is changed, but graphRoot and runRoot are not read from the configFile


**Describe the results you expected:**

The store configFile is changed and graphRoot and runRoot are read and set to the values in the configFile

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
podman version 3.2.1
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**


comments.body:
Well, after reading the code I found the option rootless_storage_path

Seems that if I'm running podman rootless, the runRoot and graphRoot are ignored when read from the configuration file, but, if I set the rootless_storage_path, then the graphRoot and volumePath changes automatically to the one specified in rootless_storage_path.

Is this the intended behavior?
This definitely sounds like a bug, we should not be ignoring that environment variable.

Well, the variable is not ignored. The configFile value is set if I set the variable. The problem is that the values I set into that alternate file for runRoot and graphRoot are not being taken into account when running the rootless podman. There is another extra variable as I told before, called rootless_storage_path that seems to do what I need, but I asked if this is the intended behavior or there is something weird here.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

Yes I believe by default we ignore the graphRoot and runRoot settings in rootless mode.  Basically those fields are for rootfull and we automatically force the settings in rootless mode to the users homedir unless rootless_storage_path is set.

@giuseppe Any other comment on this?
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
I think I answered this, so closing.  Reopen if more info is required.
